statements.summary: Supports creating a dedicated revenue stream for permanent housing and services to address homelessness via the Bring Chicago Home ordinance.
statements.quote: Support the Bring Chicago Home campaign and City Council ordinance to increase the real estate transfer tax on properties over $1 million to create a dedicated revenue stream in Chicago for permanent housing and services to address homelessness.

statements.summary: Will reform zoning to allow supportive housing and shelters by right, removing additional approval requirements.
statements.quote: Reform the Chicago Zoning Ordinance to allow supportive housing and shelters by right. We cannot afford to require additional zoning approvals for these housing types during a housing crisis.

statements.summary: Will waive fees for new affordable housing buildings and fast-track zoning and building approvals.
statements.quote: Reduce the cost to develop affordable housing by waiving fees for new affordable housing buildings, and fast-track zoning and building approvals so that affordable housing projects jump to the front of the queue.

statements.summary: Will preserve Single Room Occupancy buildings with ongoing funding.
statements.quote: Preserve the City’s Single Room Occupancy buildings, instead of letting them be converted into upscale apartments, by committing ongoing funding for SRO preservation.

statements.summary: Will enact a freeze on transferring CHA land to non-housing uses.
statements.quote: Hold the CHA accountable by immediately enacting a freeze on the transfer of CHA land to non-housing uses. We cannot allow public land intended for housing to be auctioned off to the highest bidder or to political allies.

statements.summary: Will withhold funding for CHA projects not directly leading to housing until progress is made.
statements.quote: Withhold funding for any CHA project that does not lead directly to housing until the Authority makes significant progress towards filling vacancies and develops a plan to fulfill its public housing unit commitments in new developments.

statements.summary: Will create a homeless preference at CHA prioritizing homeless individuals for vouchers and units.
statements.quote: Create a homeless preference at CHA which prioritizes people experiencing homelessness for Housing Choice Vouchers and site-based units.

statements.summary: Will stop deferring to aldermanic privilege to veto affordable housing developments.
statements.quote: Stop deferring to aldermanic privilege when it comes to creating supportive housing. Alderpeople should be involved in development and zoning decisions in their wards, but they should not have sole veto power over affordable housing developments that serve our lowest income residents. We need more housing at every income level across the city.

statements.summary: Will push for rent stabilization legislation at state level with property tax abatements.
statements.quote: Push for rent stabilization legislation at the state level to end unreasonable rent increases in Chicago, while providing property tax abatements that could provide more stability for both renters and smaller, family-owned buildings and properties.

statements.summary: Will enact a Just Cause Eviction ordinance to ensure continuity of tenancy.
statements.quote: Enact a Just Cause Eviction ordinance to ensure continuity of tenancy for tenants in good standing.

statements.summary: Will enforce bans on discrimination by funding fair housing testers.
statements.quote: Enforce City and State bans on discriminating against potential tenants based on source of income and the Just Housing ordinances’ protections by funding fair housing testers to root out discriminatory practices and educate landlords and tenants about laws banning source of income and criminal history discrimination.

statements.summary: Will fully fund the Right to Counsel program beyond its pilot phase.
statements.quote: Fully fund the City’s Right to Counsel program beyond its pilot phase, which provides funds for free legal services for tenants facing eviction. Using City resources to keep tenants in their homes and prevent homelessness saves the City money and resources in the long run.

statements.summary: Will expand the Additional Dwelling Unit pilot to all areas of the city.
statements.quote: Expand the city's Additional Dwelling Unit pilot to all areas of the city to allow people to create units in basements, attics and coach houses.

statements.summary: Will create a Chicago Social Housing Pilot Program for mixed-income housing capped at 30% of income.
statements.quote: Create a Chicago Social Housing Pilot Program to fund, acquire and/or develop mixed-income social housing buildings in desirable locations close to transit, parks and recreation, capped at 30% of a tenant’s income going towards rent.

statements.summary: Will push for rent stabilization with property tax abatements.
statements.quote: Push for rent stabilization legislation at the state level to end unreasonable rent increases in Chicago, while providing property tax abatements that could provide more stability for both renters and smaller, family-owned buildings and properties.

statements.summary: Will instruct buildings department to review regulatory changes to reduce costs.
statements.quote: Instruct the buildings department to review regulatory changes that may make it cheaper to build multi-story housing without compromising safety.
